title: Velvety Kenyan Freekeh Tagine
description: A funky and romantic Kenyan dish featuring freekeh, cooked to perfection in a tagine, suitable for diabetics. This dish is a unique blend of African and Middle Eastern flavors.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken breast pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the freekeh, chicken broth, and dried apricots, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees for 25 minutes.
Remove the tagine or Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the velvety Kenyan freekeh tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
